commit.message:
fix(bossclaw): render wechat qr as image (#2496)

## Summary
- Render Bossclaw WeChat setup QR codes as real PNG image elements so
mobile long-press recognition works.
- Keep inline backend QR image URLs as direct image sources.
- Add unit coverage for generated and inline QR image rendering paths.

## Test plan
- `bash scripts/verify-web.sh
web/app/src/app/[locale]/bossclaw/components/WechatBindStep.tsx
web/app/src/app/[locale]/bossclaw/bossclaw.module.css
web/app/tests/unit/bossclaw/wechat-bind-step.unit.spec.tsx`
- Manual local smoke: `http://localhost:3000/zh/bossclaw` responds 200
with current branch running.

Co-authored-by: Developer <dev@srp.one>

commit.message:
fix(deploy): pass bossclaw agent env (#2495)

## Summary
- Pass `NEXT_PUBLIC_BOSSCLAW_AGENT_ID` from GitHub environment variables
into the web deploy environment.
- Include the variable in the generated frontend `.env` file and
Cloudflare `wrangler deploy --var` runtime vars.
- Validate the variable during deploy so dev, staging, and production
fail early if it is missing.

## Test plan
- `git diff --check --cached` before commit
- Workflow-only change; no frontend/backend runtime tests run.

Co-authored-by: Developer <dev@srp.one>

commit.message:
fix(enterprise-admin): keep incomplete org onboarding on setup (#2492)

## Summary
- Keep incomplete enterprise-admin orgs on onboarding Step 1 so admins
can review and edit the org name/logo before continuing.
- Update Step 1 for existing incomplete orgs via `POST /orgs/{org_id}`
instead of creating a new org.
- Expose `logo_url` in `/account/me` org context and allow org `name` in
`OrgUpdateRequest`.

## Root cause
The split onboarding flow treated `registration_completed=false` as
meaning Step 1 was already complete and mapped the initial onboarding
step directly to `invite-team`. That skipped the only UI where admins
can edit org name and logo. The `/account/me` org projection also did
not expose `logo_url`, and backend org updates did not accept `name`, so
the frontend could not safely prefill and persist those Step 1 edits.

## Test plan
- [x] `bash scripts/verify-changed.sh` (reported no locally verifiable
surfaces changed vs `origin/main`)
- [x] `pnpm --dir web/enterprise-admin exec vitest run
app/onboarding/__tests__/onboarding-page.test.tsx`
- [x] `pnpm --dir web/enterprise-admin exec tsc --noEmit`
- [x] `pnpm --dir web/enterprise-admin exec eslint
app/onboarding/useOnboardingViewModel.ts
app/onboarding/__tests__/onboarding-page.test.tsx types/org.ts
types/user-me.ts`

commit.message:
feat(bossclaw): one-off onboarding wizard with post-signup agent auto-install (#2412)

## Linear

https://linear.app/srpone/issue/ECA-983/bossclaw-one-off-onboarding-flow-with-dedicated-warm-pool

## Summary
- **V3 design — zero backend changes**: bossclaw users are plain ECAP
users on the shared warm pool; the only channel difference is the page
auto-installing a configured agent (`NEXT_PUBLIC_BOSSCLAW_AGENT_ID`)
after signup via the existing hire pipeline (`install/async` + operation
polling).
- Standalone mobile-first wizard at `app/[locale]/bossclaw`
(self-contained for one-shot removal): Firebase phone login + Turnstile
→ optional subscription-code redemption (existing
`/api/gift-code/redeem`) → WeChat-binding QR bound to the installed
agent (personal WeChat binds exactly one agent). The install runs in the
background during the subscription-code step; the WeChat step waits for
it with a retry path.
- Spec v3
(`docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-11-bossclaw-onboarding-design.md`)
records the design evolution and the three accepted trade-offs: no
dedicated 100-bot pool (bump `WARM_POOL_TARGET_SIZE` for the campaign
window), agent installs ~30–60s after signup, trial credits granted
normally.
- Supersedes #2405 (v2 pool-segmentation approach, closed); the
companion user-interface PR #138 is closed unmerged since `role` is no
longer touched.

**Deploy config**: frontend `NEXT_PUBLIC_BOSSCLAW_AGENT_ID`; ops bumps
`WARM_POOL_TARGET_SIZE` during the campaign. Teardown = revert the env
bump + delete the `bossclaw/` directory.

## Test plan
- [x] Frontend: 7164 unit tests passed (new: install state machine +
wizard resume), `tsc --noEmit` + eslint clean
- [ ] Staging smoke: signup → agent auto-install completes → WeChat QR
binds to the agent

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

## Size override justification
- This PR is intentionally self-contained for the BossClaw one-off
onboarding surface: route, wizard state, login/redeem/WeChat binding UI,
agent auto-install glue, and targeted unit coverage must ship together
for staging smoke testing.
- The largest added file is the dedicated BossClaw CSS module; keeping
the styling local avoids touching shared design-system surfaces and
keeps teardown to deleting the bossclaw directory.
- Follow-up fixes in this pass add regression tests for QR rendering,
UID-scoped wizard progress, partial-login handling, and SMS OTP request
shape.

---------

Co-authored-by: Developer <dev@srp.one>

files.patch:
@@ -104,3 +104,7 @@ OPENCLAW_AGENT_DOWNLOAD_GITHUB_PAT=
 # ── Backend: AI Services ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
 GEMINI_API_KEY=
 E2B_API_KEY=
+
+# ── Bossclaw campaign (one-off onboarding page; agent auto-installed after signup) ──
+# Phone login uses Aliyun SMS via account-service (/auth/sms/*); no Firebase here.
+NEXT_PUBLIC_BOSSCLAW_AGENT_ID=

@@ -0,0 +1,180 @@ +# Bossclaw 一次性注册引导流程 — 设计 Spec + +日期：2026-06-11（v3 重写：2026-06-12） +状态：v3 已确认方向，待按 v3 精简实现 +分支 / worktree：`feature/bossclaw-onboarding`（`.worktrees/bossclaw-onboarding`） +相关：ecap-workspace PR #2405 · Linear ECA-983 + +> **v3 核心转向（2026-06-12 决策）**：不再做专用 warmpool 分池，不复用/不触碰 +> account-service 的 `role` 字段，bossclaw 渠道用户与普通 ECAP 用户**完全同构**—— +> 唯一差异是注册成功后由活动页面**调用现有 hire 接口自动安装指定 agent**。 +> 核心动机：与原有链路零冲突、零新增后端复杂度。 +> v2 的分池设计（pool_tag / 专池 TTL / 认领前预装 / role 选池）已否决，细节见本文件 +> git 历史与文末决策记录；对应已实现代码将从 PR #2405 中移除，user-interface +> PR #138 关闭不合并。 + +## 1. 背景与目标 + +为一次性拉新活动提供"扫码即用"体验：用户扫入口二维码 → 手机号验证码注册 → +秒级获得可用 bot（共享 warmpool）→ 页面自动为其安装指定 agent → 填订阅码获得权益 +→ 得到微信绑定二维码，截图后在微信里扫码，把自己的微信绑到该 agent 上。 + +需求（v3 口径）： + +1. 用户登录进来直接有可用 pod —— 复用现有共享 warmpool；活动期把 + `WARM_POOL_TARGET_SIZE` 调大以承接扫码峰值，活动结束调回。**不建专用池**。 +2. 国内手机号验证码登录 —— **改用阿里云短信（Dypnsapi 云端托管验证码）**，account-service + 新增 `/auth/sms/send` + `/auth/sms/verify`（user-interface PR #140）。发送/校验全在 + 服务端，浏览器只连 account-service（国内可达），**彻底去掉 Firebase、reCAPTCHA、 + Turnstile、channel 免审**——这些都是 Firebase 浏览器直连 Google 才需要的。 + verify 直接返回业务 JWT，不经过 `/auth/exchange`，与邮箱 OTP 同构、同样认领 warmpool。 +3. 预装 agent —— 改为**注册后自动 hire**：页面调现有 + `POST /api/openclaw/agents/install/async` 安装运营配置的 agent（约 30–60s， + 用订阅码步骤垫时间）。 +4. 微信绑定二维码 —— 复用 `weixin/setup` + `poll`，绑定到刚安装的 agent + （个人微信一个账号只绑一个 agent，目标 agent 由前端配置指定）。 +5. 订阅码 —— 复用 `POST /api/gift-code/redeem`，页面默认展示、可跳过。 +6. 前端页面独立开发（一次性、移动端优先）；**后端零改动**。 + +### v3 明确接受的三个需求反转 + +| v2 需求 | v3 变化 | 理由 | +|---------|---------|------| +| 预配 ~100 个专用 bot、3 天未认领回收 | 取消。共享池按需补货，活动前调大 target | 没有专池就没有专池回收问题 | +| 认领时 agent 已就位 | 注册后异步安装（30–60s），wizard 内等待 | 体感差异小，换来零后端改动 | +| 活动用户不发试用积分 | 照发（无渠道标记，无从区分） | "不特殊处理"的代价，运营已接受 | + +## 2. 现状盘点（v3 下全部为复用，零新增） + +| 能力 | 复用方式 | +|------|----------| +| Warmpool 秒级认领 | 现状原样：注册时 `maybe_finalize_warm_pool_registration()` 认领共享池条目；活动期仅调 `WARM_POOL_TARGET_SIZE` 环境变量 | +| 手机号登录 | 阿里云短信 → `/auth/sms/send` + `/auth/sms/verify`（云端托管验证码，PR #140，无 captcha/Firebase）→ `/users/create` | +| 安装 agent | 现有 hire 流水线：`POST /api/openclaw/agents/install/async`（202 + operation 轮询，`GET /api/openclaw/agents/operations/{id}`）；前置条件 bot ready（`GET /openclaw/status` 轮询，warmpool 认领的 bot 即刻 ready） | +| 微信绑定 | 现状原样：`POST /openclaw/settings/channels/weixin/setup`（带 `agent_id`）+ `GET .../poll` + cancel；QR 为微信返回的图片 URL，5 分钟过期 | +| 订阅码 | 现状原样：`POST /api/gift-code/redeem`；运营用 `POST /admin/subscription-codes` 批量生成 | + +## 3. 用户旅程 + +```mermaid +flowchart TD + A["扫入口二维码"] --> B["打开 /bossclaw 移动端页面"] + B --> C["输入手机号 (+86, 无验证码图)"] + C --> D["阿里云短信验证码"] + D --> E["注册成功: 共享 warmpool 秒领 bot"] + E --> F["页面后台自动安装配置的 agent"] + F --> G["填订阅码 (可跳过, 同时垫安装等待时间)"] + G --> H["展示微信绑定二维码 (绑定该 agent)"] + H --> I["用户截图, 微信内扫码确认"] + I --> J["完成页: 提示去微信和 agent 对话"] +``` + +安装（F）在订阅码步骤（G）期间后台进行；进入微信绑定（H）前若安装未完成， +显示"正在配置你的专属 agent…"等待态。 + +## 4. 模块设计 + +### 4.1 Warmpool —— 不分池，仅活动期扩容 + +- 共享池逻辑零改动。活动上线前把 `WARM_POOL_TARGET_SIZE` 从 5 调到预估峰值 + （如 20–50），必要时同步调大 `WARM_POOL_PROVISION_BATCH` 与 cron 频率； + 活动结束调回，多余 ready 条目随现有 `expires_at` 清理自然回收。 +- 风险：扫码高峰可能抽干池子，后续用户退化为普通注册路径（现场建 bot，等几分钟） + —— 用调大 target 缓解，属运营容量问题而非代码问题。 + +### 4.2 登录 —— 阿里云短信（手机号）+ EngageLab（邮箱） + +- 手机号登录走阿里云短信（`/auth/sms/send` + `/auth/sms/verify`，PR #140）：阿里云 + 云端生成/存储/校验验证码，前端无 Firebase、无 reCAPTCHA、无 Turnstile，浏览器只连 + account-service。verify 直接返回业务 JWT（不经过 `/auth/exchange`），**不传 role** + （`role` 字段保留给 gem 的 creator 多账号语义，ECAP 不沾）。 +- 邮箱验证码登录（EngageLab）作为第二渠道并列保留，链路同构（也不经过 `/auth/exchange`）。 +- 一个手机号天然只有一个账号（与主站同链路），不存在双账号问题。 + +### 4.3 注册后自动安装 agent（替代"认领前预装"） + +- 页面登录成功后： + 1. 轮询 `GET /openclaw/status` 至 bot ready（warmpool 认领时即刻 ready； + 池子抽干退化场景下等待现场创建）； + 2. 调 `POST /api/openclaw/agents/install/async`，`agent_id` 取自前端配置 + `NEXT_PUBLIC_BOSSCLAW_AGENT_ID`（official catalog 中的 agent）， + `source: "official"`； + 3. 轮询 `GET /api/openclaw/agents/operations/{operation_id}` 至完成； + 失败可重试（hire 流水线幂等）。 +- 安装与订阅码步骤并行（后台进行），仅在进入微信绑定前强制等待完成。 +- 微信绑定目标 = 同一个 `NEXT_PUBLIC_BOSSCLAW_AGENT_ID`（个人微信一个账号只绑 + 一个 agent）。如运营要预装多个 agent，扩展为逗号分隔列表依次 install， + 微信仍只绑列表第一个 —— 首版按单个实现（YAGNI）。 + +### 4.4 微信绑定二维码 + +- 复用 `weixin/setup`（请求体 `agent_id=`）+ + `poll` + `cancel`。QR 为微信 iLink 返回的图片 URL，直接 `` 展示， + 300 秒倒计时，过期一键重新生成；每 5 秒 poll，`success` 后进完成页。 +- 通道写入需要 bot running —— warmpool bot 始终 running，满足。 + +### 4.5 订阅码 + +- 零改动复用 `POST /api/gift-code/redeem`，错误码映射照抄 `UserMenu.tsx`。 +- 页面默认展示输入框、可跳过（未兑码 = free 计划 + 正常试用积分）。 +- 运营提前用 `POST /admin/subscription-codes` 批量生成（单次使用）。 + +### 4.6 前端一次性页面 + +- 路由：`web/app/src/app/[locale]/bossclaw/`，独立布局、组件自包含、整目录可删； + 移动端优先（375px 起）。 +- 四步 wizard：①手机号登录 → ②订阅码（后台并行装 agent）→ ③微信绑定 QR → ④完成。 +- 步骤可恢复：登录态 + localStorage 步骤标记，刷新不丢进度；agent 安装状态以 + 服务端为准（operations 轮询幂等）。 +- 复用主站 `apiClient`/localStorage 约定，用户后续可无缝登录主站。 +- 登录走 account-service 的 `/auth/sms/*`（手机号）与 `/auth/email-otp/*`（邮箱）， + 均无需 Firebase / Turnstile；install/operations、订阅码、weixin 的路由全部已存在。 +- 入口二维码：指向页面 URL 的静态二维码，运营自行生成。 + +## 5. 改动汇总（v3） + +| 位置 | 改动 | +|------|------| +| `web/app/src/app/[locale]/bossclaw/` | wizard 页面：去掉 role 传参；新增 agent 安装状态机（status 轮询 → install/async → operations 轮询）；微信步前等待安装完成 | +| `web/app/src/lib/auth/api.ts` / `manager.ts` | 新增 `sendSmsOTP`/`verifySmsOTP` + `loginWithSmsOTP`；PhoneLoginStep 改为阿里云短信实现 | +| `user-interface`（PR #140） | 新增 `/auth/sms/send` + `/auth/sms/verify` + `aliyun_sms.py`（手机号登录唯一的后端改动） | +| `services/claw-interface` | **零改动** | +| 部署配置 | 活动期调大 `WARM_POOL_TARGET_SIZE`；前端配置 `NEXT_PUBLIC_BOSSCLAW_AGENT_ID` | + +## 6. 需要的权限 / 资源 + +1. **阿里云号码认证服务（Dypnsapi）**：开通服务 + 申请短信签名（SignName）+ 验证码模板 + （TemplateCode，内容须含 `##code##` 占位符）+ RAM 子账号 AccessKey；配进 account-service + 的 `ALIYUN_SMS_*` secret。 +2. 微信 iLink 沿用现状，无新申请。 +3. 活动期共享池扩容的容量确认（`WARM_POOL_TARGET_SIZE` 调大 → 常驻 pod 增加， + 规模远小于 v2 的 100 专池）。 +4. 管理后台批量生成订阅码（已有接口）。 + +## 7. 决策记录 + +1. **2026-06-12 / v2**：曾一度复用 Firebase 短信；后确认其 SMS 发送+reCAPTCHA 是浏览器 + 直连 Google，国内需 VPN 且触发图形验证。**2026-06-15 改回阿里云短信（Dypnsapi 托管验证）** + 彻底解决（PR #140）。 +2. **2026-06-12 / v2**：微信绑定目标 agent 独立配置；个人微信一账号一 agent。 +3. **2026-06-12 / v2→v3**：确认"一个手机号只能一个账号"后，发现 role 是 gem + creator 多账号语义（PR #89），ECAP 复用它需要翻转 PR #110 契约（曾做出 + user-interface PR #138）。 +4. **2026-06-12 / v3（最终）**：彻底不分池、不用 role、不做任何渠道侧后端特殊 + 处理——bossclaw 用户 = 普通 ECAP 用户 + 页面自动 hire 一个 agent。接受三个 + 需求反转（见 §1 表格）。PR #138 关闭；PR #2405 移除全部后端改动。 + +## 8. 测试计划 + +- 前端单测：wizard 步骤恢复、agent 安装状态机（status/install/operations 轮询的 + 状态转换）；`pnpm test:unit` 全量回归。 +- 手动：阿里云测试号走全流程；重测循环 = `DELETE /user/me`（account-service， + 软删释放手机号）+ 清 localStorage（旧 uid 的 bot 资源不回收，已确认接受）。 +- staging 冒烟：扫码 → 注册 → agent 安装完成 → 微信绑定成功。 + +## 9. 上线与回收 + +- 上线：调大 `WARM_POOL_TARGET_SIZE` + 配置 `NEXT_PUBLIC_BOSSCLAW_AGENT_ID` + + 生成订阅码 + 发布前端。 +- 回收：`WARM_POOL_TARGET_SIZE` 调回 + 一个 PR 删除 `bossclaw/` 目录。后端无任何 + 活动残留代码。

@@ -0,0 +1,116 @@
+import { getComputerStatus, getOpenClawStatus, initOpenClaw, listComputers } from '@/lib/api/openclaw'
+import type { OpenClawAgentResponse } from '@/lib/api/openclaw-agents-v2-types'
+import {
+  installAgentOnComputer,
+  listAllAgentsOnComputer,
+  waitForAgentWorkspaceStatus,
+} from '@/services/agent-workspaces'
+
+export type InstallPhase = 'idle' | 'waiting-bot' | 'installing' | 'done' | 'error'
+
+type InstallPlan = 'skip' | 'wait' | 'install'
+type BossclawWorkspace = Pick<OpenClawAgentResponse, 'agent_id' | 'status'>
+
+const BOT_POLL_INTERVAL_MS = 3000
+const COMPUTER_POLL_INTERVAL_MS = 3000
+const INSTALL_TIMEOUT_MS = 300_000
+// ~5 min: covers the cold bot-creation path when the shared warm pool is drained.
+const BOT_POLL_MAX_ATTEMPTS = 100
+const COMPUTER_POLL_MAX_ATTEMPTS = 100
+const TERMINAL_BOT_STATUSES = new Set(['error', 'expired'])
+
+export function resolveInstallPlan(workspaces: BossclawWorkspace[], targetId: string): InstallPlan {
+  if (!targetId) return 'skip'
+
+  const workspace = workspaces.find((agent) => agent.agent_id === targetId)
+  if (workspace?.status === 'active') return 'skip'
+  if (workspace?.status === 'installing') return 'wait'
+  return 'install'
+}
+
+function sleep(ms: number): Promise<void> {
+  return new Promise((resolve) => setTimeout(resolve, ms))
+}
+
+function throwIfTerminalBotStatus(status: { status: string; message?: string }): void {
+  if (TERMINAL_BOT_STATUSES.has(status.status)) {
+    throw new Error(status.message || 'bot not ready')
+  }
+}
+
+async function ensureBossclawBotReady(uid: string): Promise<void> {
+  const status = await getOpenClawStatus(uid)
+  if (status.status === 'expired') {
+    throw new Error(status.message || 'bot not ready')
+  }
+  if (status.status === 'ready') return
+
+  const init = await initOpenClaw(uid, { selected_agent_ids: [] })
+  if (init.status === 'ready') return
+  throwIfTerminalBotStatus(init)
+
+  for (let attempt = 0; attempt < BOT_POLL_MAX_ATTEMPTS; attempt++) {
+    await sleep(BOT_POLL_INTERVAL_MS)
+    const pollStatus = await getOpenClawStatus(uid)
+    if (pollStatus.status === 'ready') return
+    throwIfTerminalBotStatus(pollStatus)
+  }
+
+  throw new Error(init.message || status.message || 'bot not ready')
+}
+
+async function resolveReadyComputerId(): Promise<string> {
+  const { computers } = await listComputers()
+  const readyComputerId = computers.find((computer) => computer.status === 'ready' && computer.computer_id)?.computer_id
+  if (readyComputerId) return readyComputerId
+
+  const candidateComputerId = computers.find((computer) => computer.computer_id)?.computer_id
+  if (!candidateComputerId) {
+    throw new Error('computer not ready')
+  }
+
+  for (let attempt = 0; attempt < COMPUTER_POLL_MAX_ATTEMPTS; attempt++) {
+    const status = await getComputerStatus(candidateComputerId)
+    if (status.ready === true || status.status === 'ready') return candidateComputerId
+    await sleep(COMPUTER_POLL_INTERVAL_MS)
+  }
+
+  throw new Error('computer not ready')
+}
+
+async function waitForBossclawWorkspace(computerId: string, targetId: string): Promise<void> {
+  await waitForAgentWorkspaceStatus(computerId, targetId, {
+    completeStatuses: ['active'],
+    failedStatuses: ['install_failed', 'error'],
+    timeoutMs: INSTALL_TIMEOUT_MS,
+  })
+}
+
+export async function ensureBossclawAgent(
+  uid: string,
+  targetId: string,
+  onPhase: (phase: InstallPhase) => void,
+): Promise<void> {
+  if (!targetId) {
+    onPhase('error')
+    throw new Error('NEXT_PUBLIC_BOSSCLAW_AGENT_ID is not set')
+  }
+
+  onPhase('waiting-bot')
+  await ensureBossclawBotReady(uid)
+  const computerId = await resolveReadyComputerId()
+  const workspaces = await listAllAgentsOnComputer(computerId)
+  const plan = resolveInstallPlan(workspaces, targetId)
+
+  if (plan === 'skip') {
+    onPhase('done')
+    return
+  }
+
+  onPhase('installing')
+  if (plan === 'install') {
+    await installAgentOnComputer(computerId, targetId, { source: 'official' })
+  }
+  await waitForBossclawWorkspace(computerId, targetId)
+  onPhase('done')
+}

+import { afterEach, beforeEach, describe, expect, it, vi } from 'vitest'
+
+const apiMocks = vi.hoisted(() => ({
+  initOpenClaw: vi.fn(),
+  getOpenClawStatus: vi.fn(),
+  listComputers: vi.fn(),
+  getComputerStatus: vi.fn(),
+}))
+vi.mock('@/lib/api/openclaw', () => apiMocks)
+
+const workspaceMocks = vi.hoisted(() => ({
+  installAgentOnComputer: vi.fn(),
+  listAllAgentsOnComputer: vi.fn(),
+  waitForAgentWorkspaceStatus: vi.fn(),
+}))
+vi.mock('@/services/agent-workspaces', () => workspaceMocks)
+
+import { ensureBossclawAgent, resolveInstallPlan } from '@/app/[locale]/bossclaw/agent-install'
+
+describe('resolveInstallPlan', () => {
+  it('skips when no target configured', () => {
+    expect(resolveInstallPlan([], '')).toBe('skip')
+  })
+
+  it('skips when workspace is already active', () => {
+    expect(resolveInstallPlan([{ agent_id: 'boss_agent', status: 'active' }], 'boss_agent')).toBe('skip')
+  })
+
+  it('waits when workspace install is already in progress', () => {
+    expect(resolveInstallPlan([{ agent_id: 'boss_agent', status: 'installing' }], 'boss_agent')).toBe('wait')
+  })
+
+  it('installs when workspace is missing or failed', () => {
+    expect(resolveInstallPlan([{ agent_id: 'other', status: 'active' }], 'boss_agent')).toBe('install')
+    expect(resolveInstallPlan([{ agent_id: 'boss_agent', status: 'install_failed' }], 'boss_agent')).toBe('install')
+  })
+})
+
+describe('ensureBossclawAgent', () => {
+  beforeEach(() => {
+    vi.clearAllMocks()
+    apiMocks.getOpenClawStatus.mockResolvedValue({ status: 'ready', bot: { bot_id: 'bot-1' }, message: '' })
+    apiMocks.initOpenClaw.mockResolvedValue({ status: 'ready', bot: { bot_id: 'bot-1' }, message: '' })
+    apiMocks.listComputers.mockResolvedValue({
+      computers: [{ computer_id: 'computer-1', computer_name: 'Boss computer', status: 'ready' }],
+      total: 1,
+    })
+    apiMocks.getComputerStatus.mockResolvedValue({ ready: true, status: 'ready' })
+    workspaceMocks.listAllAgentsOnComputer.mockResolvedValue([])
+    workspaceMocks.installAgentOnComputer.mockResolvedValue({ agent_id: 'boss_agent', status: 'installing' })

+    expect(workspaceMocks.waitForAgentWorkspaceStatus).toHaveBeenCalledWith('computer-1', 'boss_agent', {

+})

+import { cleanup, fireEvent, render, screen, waitFor } from '@testing-library/react'
+import { afterEach, beforeEach, describe, expect, it, vi } from 'vitest'
+
+const apiMocks = vi.hoisted(() => ({ sendSmsOTP: vi.fn() }))
+const managerMocks = vi.hoisted(() => ({ loginWithSmsOTP: vi.fn() }))
+vi.mock('@/lib/auth/api', () => apiMocks)
+vi.mock('@/lib/auth/manager', () => ({ authManager: managerMocks }))
+
+import PhoneLoginStep from '@/app/[locale]/bossclaw/components/PhoneLoginStep'
+
+describe('PhoneLoginStep (Aliyun SMS)', () => {
+  beforeEach(() => {
+    vi.clearAllMocks()
+    apiMocks.sendSmsOTP.mockResolvedValue({ ok: true })
+    managerMocks.loginWithSmsOTP.mockResolvedValue({ uid: 'u1' })
+  })
+
+  afterEach(() => cleanup())
+
+  it('disables send until the phone is a valid +86 mobile', () => {
+    render(<PhoneLoginStep onNext={vi.fn()} />)
+    const sendButton = screen.getByRole('button', { name: '获取验证码' })
+    expect(sendButton).toBeDisabled()
+    fireEvent.change(screen.getByPlaceholderText('请输入手机号'), { target: { value: '13800138000' } })
+    expect(sendButton).not.toBeDisabled()
+  })
+
+  it('sends the code via Aliyun then verifies and advances', async () => {
+    const onNext = vi.fn()
+    render(<PhoneLoginStep onNext={onNext} />)
+    fireEvent.change(screen.getByPlaceholderText('请输入手机号'), { target: { value: '13800138000' } })
+    fireEvent.click(screen.getByRole('button', { name: '获取验证码' }))
+
+    await waitFor(() => expect(screen.getByPlaceholderText('6 位验证码')).toBeInTheDocument())
+    expect(apiMocks.sendSmsOTP).toHaveBeenCalledWith('+8613800138000')
+
+    fireEvent.change(screen.getByPlaceholderText('6 位验证码'), { target: { value: '123456' } })
+    fireEvent.click(screen.getByRole('button', { name: '登录' }))
+
+    await waitFor(() => expect(onNext).toHaveBeenCalled())
+    expect(managerMocks.loginWithSmsOTP).toHaveBeenCalledWith('+8613800138000', '123456')
+  })
+
+  it('surfaces the backend reason on send failure', async () => {
+    apiMocks.sendSmsOTP.mockRejectedValue(new Error('rate limited'))
+    render(<PhoneLoginStep onNext={vi.fn()} />)
+    fireEvent.change(screen.getByPlaceholderText('请输入手机号'), { target: { value: '13800138000' } })
+    fireEvent.click(screen.getByRole('button', { name: '获取验证码' }))
+
+    await waitFor(() => expect(screen.getByText('短信发送失败：rate limited')).toBeInTheDocument())
+    expect(managerMocks.loginWithSmsOTP).not.toHaveBeenCalled()
+  })
+
+  it('shows an error when verification fails', async () => {
+    managerMocks.loginWithSmsOTP.mockRejectedValue(new Error('bad code'))
+    const onNext = vi.fn()
+    render(<PhoneLoginStep onNext={onNext} />)
+    fireEvent.change(screen.getByPlaceholderText('请输入手机号'), { target: { value: '13800138000' } })
+    fireEvent.click(screen.getByRole('button', { name: '获取验证码' }))
+    await waitFor(() => screen.getByPlaceholderText('6 位验证码'))
+    fireEvent.change(screen.getByPlaceholderText('6 位验证码'), { target: { value: '000000' } })
+    fireEvent.click(screen.getByRole('button', { name: '登录' }))
+
+    await waitFor(() => expect(screen.getByText('登录失败：bad code')).toBeInTheDocument())
+    expect(onNext).not.toHaveBeenCalled()
+  })
+
+  it('does not advance when login resolves without a uid', async () => {
+    managerMocks.loginWithSmsOTP.mockResolvedValue({ uid: '' })
+    const onNext = vi.fn()
+    render(<PhoneLoginStep onNext={onNext} />)
+    fireEvent.change(screen.getByPlaceholderText('请输入手机号'), { target: { value: '13800138000' } })
+    fireEvent.click(screen.getByRole('button', { name: '获取验证码' }))
+    await waitFor(() => screen.getByPlaceholderText('6 位验证码'))
+    fireEvent.change(screen.getByPlaceholderText('6 位验证码'), { target: { value: '123456' } })
+    fireEvent.click(screen.getByRole('button', { name: '登录' }))
+
+    await waitFor(() => expect(screen.getByText('登录状态同步失败，请重新登录')).toBeInTheDocument())
+    expect(onNext).not.toHaveBeenCalled()
+  })
+})

+import { cleanup, fireEvent, render, screen, waitFor } from '@testing-library/react'

+import { beforeEach, describe, expect, it } from 'vitest'
+
+import {
+  createBossclawStepPayload,
+  getBossclawStepKey,
+  parseBossclawStep,
+  readBossclawStep,
+  resolveInitialStep,
+  saveBossclawStep,
+} from '@/app/[locale]/bossclaw/wizard-state'
+import { isLoggedInUserInfo } from '@/lib/auth/storage'
+
+describe('bossclaw wizard resume', () => {
+  beforeEach(() => {
+    localStorage.clear()
+  })
+
+  it('starts at the first intro page when not authenticated', () => {
+    expect(resolveInitialStep(false, null)).toBe('intro')
+  })
+
+  it('resumes saved step when logged in', () => {
+    expect(resolveInitialStep(true, 'wechat')).toBe('wechat')
+  })
+
+  it('resumes saved step before the ttl expires', () => {
+    const savedStep = createBossclawStepPayload('wechat', 1_000)
+
+    expect(parseBossclawStep(savedStep, 1_000 + 9 * 60 * 1000)).toBe('wechat')
+  })
+
+  it('expires saved step after ten minutes', () => {
+    const savedStep = createBossclawStepPayload('wechat', 1_000)
+
+    expect(parseBossclawStep(savedStep, 1_000 + 10 * 60 * 1000)).toBeNull()
+  })
+
+  it('saves progress with a ten minute ttl', () => {
+    saveBossclawStep('user-a', 'wechat', 1_000)
+    const stored = localStorage.getItem(getBossclawStepKey('user-a'))
+
+    expect(parseBossclawStep(stored, 1_000 + 9 * 60 * 1000)).toBe('wechat')
+    expect(parseBossclawStep(stored, 1_000 + 10 * 60 * 1000)).toBeNull()
+  })
+
+  it('migrates legacy saved progress into an expiring payload', () => {
+    localStorage.setItem(getBossclawStepKey('user-a'), 'wechat')
+
+    expect(readBossclawStep('user-a', 1_000)).toBe('wechat')
+    expect(localStorage.getItem(getBossclawStepKey('user-a'))).toBe(createBossclawStepPayload('wechat', 1_000))
+  })
+
+  it('falls back to redeem when logged in without saved step', () => {
+    expect(resolveInitialStep(true, null)).toBe('redeem')
+  })
+
+  it('ignores saved progress without authenticated phone login', () => {
+    expect(resolveInitialStep(false, 'wechat')).toBe('intro')
+  })
+
+  it('keeps guest sessions with an access token at phone login', () => {
+    const guestLoggedIn = isLoggedInUserInfo({
+      type: '2',
+      access_token: 'guest-token',
+      phone_number: '',
+      email: '',
+    })
+
+    expect(guestLoggedIn).toBe(false)
+    expect(resolveInitialStep(guestLoggedIn, 'wechat')).toBe('intro')
+  })
+
+  it('never resumes into pre-redeem steps while logged in', () => {
+    expect(resolveInitialStep(true, 'intro')).toBe('redeem')
+    expect(resolveInitialStep(true, 'capabilities')).toBe('redeem')
+    expect(resolveInitialStep(true, 'login')).toBe('redeem')
+  })
+
+  it('rejects garbage saved values', () => {
+    expect(resolveInitialStep(true, 'not-a-step')).toBe('redeem')
+  })
+
+  it('scopes saved progress by user id', () => {
+    expect(getBossclawStepKey('user-a')).toBe('bossclaw:step:user-a')
+    expect(getBossclawStepKey('user-b')).toBe('bossclaw:step:user-b')
+  })
+})

commit.message:
fix(web): make the desktop app window draggable (#2477)

## Problem

The desktop (Electron) window can't be dragged to move it. The shell
creates its `BrowserWindow` with `titleBarStyle: 'hiddenInset'`
(`desktop/main/index.ts`), which hides the native title bar — so macOS
provides no draggable region. The renderer (this web app) declared no
`-webkit-app-region: drag` area anywhere, so there was nothing to grab.

## Fix

Add a thin draggable strip across the top of the content area in
`AppLayout`, rendered **only under Electron**:

- **`useIsElectron` hook** (new) — detects the desktop shell via the
preload's `window.electronAPI`; SSR-safe (false → flips after mount,
like `useIsDesktop`). In a plain browser it's always `false`, so the
strip is never rendered there → the web build is unaffected.
- **Scoped to the content area** (not full width) so it doesn't cover
the sidebar's top-left link; verified the top 32px of the content area
has no interactive elements.
- **Inline `-webkit-app-region`** — Lightning CSS strips this
non-standard property out of compiled stylesheet rules (a class-based
version had no effect at runtime), so it's set via inline style.
`AppLayout.tsx` is already in the `react/forbid-dom-props` allowlist.

## Test

Verified in the running desktop app: the strip computes
`-webkit-app-region: drag` over the content-area top (`left:260, w:585,
h:32`), and the window drags from that band while the sidebar link stays
clickable. Browser build renders no strip.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>

@@ -0,0 +1,21 @@
+'use client'
+
+import { useEffect, useState } from 'react'
+
+/**
+ * True only when running inside the ZooClaw desktop (Electron) shell.
+ *
+ * Detected via `window.electronAPI`, which the desktop app's preload exposes
+ * (`contextBridge.exposeInMainWorld('electronAPI', …)`). Returns `false` on the
+ * first (SSR / pre-mount) render and flips after mount, mirroring the lazy
+ * pattern of `useIsDesktop` so it never causes a hydration mismatch. In a plain
+ * browser the flag stays `false`, so any desktop-only chrome (e.g. the window
+ * drag strip) is isolated to the Electron build.
+ */
+export function useIsElectron(): boolean {
+  const [isElectron, setIsElectron] = useState(false)
+  useEffect(() => {
+    setIsElectron(typeof window !== 'undefined' && Boolean((window as { electronAPI?: unknown }).electronAPI))
+  }, [])
+  return isElectron
+}

commit.message:
fix(web): initialize OpenClaw on new chat (#2490)

## Summary

- Move `OpenClawProvider` to the authenticated app layout so `/new-chat`
and `/chat` share the same lazily activated init state.
- Activate OpenClaw from `/new-chat`, then enable the vertical pack
installer once init reaches `ready`.
- Update layout coverage and add a focused new-chat view-model test for
init activation plus vertical-pack enablement.

## Tests

- `bash scripts/verify-web.sh
'web/app/src/app/[locale]/(app)/layout.tsx'
'web/app/src/app/[locale]/(app)/(chat)/layout.tsx'

- `bash scripts/verify-changed.sh`

commit.message:
refactor(enterprise-admin): auth/onboarding/checkout 迁移至 enterprise-app 交互风格 + editorial 页强制浅色 (#2473)

## 背景
延续「editorial 页太黑」的修复，进一步把 enterprise-admin 的
**登录/验证/邀请/onboarding/checkout** 全部重构为 **enterprise-app 的交互组件风格**（分栏卡片 +
圆角盒子输入 + red 按钮），并保持这些 editorial 页在系统 dark 下强制浅色。

设计
spec：`docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-15-enterprise-admin-auth-checkout-interaction-port.md`

## 改动（分阶段）
0. **editorial 页强制浅色**：`:root:has(.force-light-surface)` 在 dark
下把整文档钉回浅色（Tailwind v4 只在 :root 保留 token 覆盖，故用 :has()）；`ZooClawWordmark`
支持 `theme=auto|light|dark`；favicon 换 ZooClaw mark。
1. **Login → 分栏卡片**：抽出 `components/auth/AuthShell`（居中 rounded-2xl 卡片 +
深色 ink hero + 共享样式常量）。
2. **Verify + Join**：改用 AuthShell + boxed 输入 + red 按钮 + 共享 `OtpGrid`（6
格验证码、红色填充下划线）。
3. **Onboarding**：表单（OrgSetup/BulkInvite/WarmPool）从 hairline 改为
boxed/red；StepIndicator 红色 current。向导布局保留（enterprise-app 无 onboarding
对应）。
4. **Checkout**：force-light 卡片 + ZooClaw logo；付款
Stripe(red)/支付宝(secondary)；**购买报错时显示固定中文提示**「仅支持企业账号购买 Vertical
Plan，一个企业账号仅支持购买一个套餐」**并显示 Log out 按钮**（viewModel 增加 logout）。

## 验证
- ✅ `pnpm lint` / `tsc --noEmit` / `pnpm test`（221 tests）/ `next build`
- ✅ 本地 dark 模式截图确认：login / join 分栏卡片、onboarding 表单、checkout 报错态（中文提示 +
Log out 按钮）均为 enterprise-app 浅色风格

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

---------

Co-authored-by: David Lu <davidlu@Daviddebijibendiannao.local>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
Co-authored-by: claude[bot] <41898282+claude[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

@@ -0,0 +1,47 @@
+# Enterprise Admin — Auth + Checkout interaction port to enterprise-app
+
+**Date:** 2026-06-15
+**Branch:** `fix/enterprise-admin-editorial-surfaces-light` (continues the light-surface fix; PR scope expands)
+**Scope:** `web/enterprise-admin` — login / verify / join / onboarding + vertical-pack-plan checkout
+**Reference:** `web/enterprise-app/app/login/page.tsx` + `components/ui.tsx`
+
+## Goal
+
+Move the admin auth + checkout surfaces from the current **full-bleed editorial / hairline-input** style to **enterprise-app's interaction style**: a centered split-panel card with a dark ink hero + light form, **boxed `rounded-xl` inputs** (`bg-zc-canvas border-zc-line focus:border-zc-ink`), ported `Button` (`variant="red" size="lg"` + arrow), and `bg-zc-red-50` error pills. Product owner confirmed: adopt the split-panel card (incl. the intentional dark hero), apply to all four auth pages, **and** the checkout page.
+
+## Reference (enterprise-app login)
+
+- Centered `rounded-2xl border-zc-line bg-zc-paper shadow-card-lg` card, `max-w-5xl`, `md:grid-cols-[0.85fr_1fr]`.
+- **Left dark hero** (`bg-zc-ink text-white`, `hidden md:flex`, `min-h-[560px]`): layered gradient + 36px grid, white wordmark, mono eyebrow, `text-[34px]` headline, `text-white/70` sub, `ShieldCheck` footnote.
+- **Right light form** (`px-6 py-8 sm:px-10`, mobile shows black wordmark): mono eyebrow + `text-[30px]` heading + sub, then form.
+- **Boxed input:** `h-12 w-full rounded-xl border border-zc-line bg-zc-canvas px-3.5 text-[14px] font-medium text-zc-text outline-none transition-colors placeholder:text-zc-text-3 focus:border-zc-ink`.
+- **Label:** `font-mono text-[10px] font-bold uppercase tracking-[0.16em] text-zc-text-3`.
+- **Buttons:** submit `Button variant="red" size="lg" iconRight={ArrowRight} className="w-full justify-between"`; back `Button variant="secondary" size="lg" icon={ArrowLeft}`.
+- **Error pill:** `role="alert"` `rounded-lg bg-zc-red-50 px-3 py-2 text-[13px] font-medium text-zc-red`.
+
+## Admin current (to replace)
+
+Full-bleed `min-h-screen` + `login-ambient`/`login-grid`, big `clamp()` headline, `login-reveal`/`login-hairline` cascade, **hairline inputs** (`h-12 rounded-none border-0 border-b`), separate `/verify` OTP page (6-cell grid), multi-step onboarding (StepIndicator + Org/Invite/WarmPool forms), checkout with raw `error.message`.
+
+## Plan
+
+1. **ZooClawWordmark**: replace `forceLight` boolean with `theme?: "auto" | "light" | "dark"` (auto = `<picture>`, light = black svg, dark = white svg). Hero uses `theme="dark"`; light form/mobile uses `theme="light"`; sidebar keeps `theme="auto"`.
+2. **Login** (`app/login/page.tsx`): rebuild as the split-panel card. Admin only has email→code (no Google/phone), so the form is just the boxed email input + red Continue → routes to `/verify`. Keep `force-light-surface` on root (form is light; hero dark is by design). Keep Suspense wrapper.
+3. **Verify + Join**: wrap content in the same card shell (or a single-column variant). Keep the 6-cell OTP grid (good UX) but align its styling to the new palette; boxed inputs for email; red buttons; red-50 error pills. Join keeps its state machine.
+4. **Onboarding**: keep admin's step flow (enterprise-app has none). Swap hairline inputs → boxed `rounded-xl` inputs, ghost/red Buttons, mono labels. Frame consistent with the card aesthetic. Restyle StepIndicator to match.
+5. **Checkout** (`app/vertical-pack-plan/[planId]/checkout`): restyle to the card/boxed style + ZooClaw logo + `force-light-surface`. **Functional change**: on a purchase failure show the friendly copy
+   「仅支持企业账号购买 Vertical Plan，一个企业账号仅支持购买一个套餐」
+   — map `ApiError` business rejections (4xx) from `purchaseVerticalPackPlanCheckout` to this message; keep a generic fallback for unexpected/network errors. Show it as the page's error state (red-50 pill + heading).
+6. **Verify**: `pnpm lint` + `tsc` + `pnpm test` + `next build` green; screenshot every page (light + system-dark, since force-light must hold); update PR.
+
+## Decisions
+
+- **Dark hero panel** kept (enterprise-app signature; distinct from the earlier "整页太黑" dark-mode issue — the hero is a deliberate accent beside a light form).
+- **OTP grid** retained (nicer than enterprise-app's single code box) but restyled.
+- **Onboarding** structure unchanged (no enterprise-app reference) — only its form components are ported.
+- Shared boxed-input / label / error-pill / arrow-button patterns extracted to small local constants per file (or a shared helper) to avoid drift.
+
+## Out of scope
+
+- Dashboard pages (already migrated; keep system dark mode).
+- Backend / checkout API contract (only the client-side error copy changes).

@@ -0,0 +1,107 @@
+# enterprise-admin bilingual (zh-CN / en) i18n
+
+**Date:** 2026-06-16
+**Branch:** `feature/enterprise-admin-i18n` (stacked on `fix/enterprise-admin-editorial-surfaces-light` / PR #2473)
+**Status:** in progress
+
+## Goal
+
+Add bilingual (English + 简体中文) UI copy to `web/enterprise-admin`, which is currently
+100% hardcoded English (~100 user-facing strings). A language toggle switches all copy
+live; the choice persists across sessions.
+
+## Why this approach
+
+Three i18n precedents exist in the monorepo:
+
+| App | Pattern | Fit for admin |
+| --- | --- | --- |
+| `web/app` | heavy: `[locale]` routing + middleware + 10 locales + Context | overkill; requires renaming `app/` → `app/[locale]/`, middleware, SSR locale detection — large, invasive |
+| `web/enterprise-app` | **lightweight**: `lib/i18n.tsx` — `useLang()` + `t(key, fallback)`, localStorage-persisted, en+zh, **no URL routing**, gradual migration | **chosen** |
+| `web/enterprise-admin` | none | — |
+
+`enterprise-admin` was ported from `enterprise-app`'s design system and shares its stack
+(Next 16, App Router, flat routes, no `[locale]`). We therefore mirror **enterprise-app's
+lightweight pattern** — consistent with the standing "source of truth is enterprise-app"
+rule, and far lower risk than web/app's routed approach (no middleware, no route renames,
+no SSR locale negotiation).
+
+### Trade-off accepted
+
+- No locale in the URL → a zh session isn't shareable/deep-linkable as zh, and locale isn't
+  SSR-detected from `Accept-Language`. Acceptable for an internal admin console; matches
+  enterprise-app. Can be upgraded to routed i18n later without rewriting call sites (they
+  already go through `t()`).
+- `lang` is read from `localStorage` via `useSyncExternalStore` (server snapshot is always
+  `"en"`, so server HTML and the hydrating client's first render match — no hydration
+  mismatch and no setState-in-effect). A returning zh user sees a brief English→中文 settle
+  after hydration (one render), which is acceptable. Same-tab toggles notify a listener set;
+  cross-tab changes arrive on the `storage` event.
+
+## Design
+
+### Infra — `lib/i18n.tsx` (ported from enterprise-app)
+
+- `LangProvider` — React context holding `lang` (`"en" | "zh"`), persisted to
+  `localStorage["zooclaw.admin.lang"]` (admin-scoped key, distinct from enterprise-app's
+  `zooclaw.lang`).
+- `useLang()` → `{ lang, setLang, t }`.
+- `t(key, fallback?)` resolves **current lang → en catalog → fallback → key**. English copy
+  stays inline at every call site as the `fallback`, so:
+  - nothing ever renders a raw key,
+  - English remains the de-facto source of truth,
+  - a missing zh key silently shows English (incremental-safe).
+- `CATALOG.en` is left empty (call-site fallbacks ARE the English copy); `CATALOG.zh` holds
+  the Chinese translations. This avoids duplicating English in two places.
+
+### Provider wiring
+
+`LangProvider` wraps the app once in `app/layout.tsx` (root), inside the existing providers,
+so both the auth surfaces (login/verify/join/onboarding/checkout) and the dashboard see it.
+
+### Language toggle — `components/LanguageToggle.tsx`
+
+A compact `EN / 中` segmented control (mono, matches the editorial chrome). Placed:
+- on the dashboard top bar (`app/(dashboard)/layout.tsx` header), and
+- on the auth surfaces — in `AuthShell` (covers login/verify/join) and on
+  onboarding + checkout headers.
+
+### String migration
+
+Every hardcoded user-facing string → `t("<surface>.<key>", "<English>")`. Key namespace by
+surface: `auth.*`, `login.*`, `verify.*`, `join.*`, `onboarding.*`, `users.*`, `packs.*`,
+`org.*`, `checkout.*`, `common.*` (shared: Cancel, Save, etc.), `toast.*`.
+
+`zh` translations are written to an enterprise professional standard (North-American-enterprise
+register, not machine-translation slop), consistent with the copy professionalism pass in #2473.
+
+### Checkout gating copy (reconciled with #2469)
+
+#2469 introduced `purchaseUnavailableMessage = "Only team users can purchase vertical packs."`
+The product owner's intended copy is richer and covers two complementary cases (no semantic
+conflict with #2469):
+
+- gate (non-enterprise account): `checkout.enterpriseOnly`
+  - en: "Only enterprise accounts can purchase a Vertical Plan."
+  - zh: "仅支持企业账号购买 Vertical Plan"
+- one-plan limit (a backend error case, shown via `vm.error`): `checkout.onePlanLimit`
+  - en: "Each enterprise account can purchase only one plan."
+  - zh: "一个企业账号仅支持购买一个套餐"
+
+The viewModel stops hardcoding the English message; the component renders the translated key.
+#2469's checkout test is updated to assert the reconciled gate copy.
+
+## Testing
+
+- `lib/__tests__/i18n.test.tsx`: `t()` resolution order (zh hit / en fallback / call-site
+  fallback / key passthrough); `setLang` persists to localStorage; provider re-renders.
+- `components/__tests__/LanguageToggle.test.tsx`: toggling updates `useLang().lang`.
+- Existing tests keep asserting English — they don't mount with zh, so `t()` returns the
+  English fallback unchanged. Only #2469's checkout-gate assertion is updated to the
+  reconciled English copy.
+
+## Out of scope
+
+- URL-routed locales / SSR `Accept-Language` detection (future upgrade).
+- Locales beyond en/zh.
+- Backend-returned error message translation (shown as-is; only first-party UI copy is localized).

commit.message:
fix(web): forward session cookie auth to backend proxy (#2489)

## Summary
- Forward the HttpOnly `zc_session` token as `Authorization` when
`proxyToBackend` receives an authenticated request without an explicit
bearer header.
- Add regression coverage for cookie fallback and explicit bearer
precedence.

## Root cause
The middleware can authenticate API requests from the `zc_session`
cookie, but `proxyToBackend` only forwarded an existing `Authorization`
header to claw-interface. Cookie-authenticated BFF requests therefore
passed middleware while the backend still received no bearer token.

## Test plan
- [x] `pnpm --dir web/app exec vitest run
tests/unit/lib/api/proxy.unit.spec.ts`
- [x] `bash scripts/verify-web.sh web/app/src/lib/api/proxy.ts
web/app/tests/unit/lib/api/proxy.unit.spec.ts`
- [x] `bash scripts/verify-changed.sh`

`proxyToBackend` receives an authenticated request without an explicit
bearer header.
- Add regression coverage for cookie fallback and explicit bearer
precedence.

## Root cause
The middleware can authenticate API requests from the `zc_session`
cookie, but `proxyToBackend` only forwarded an existing `Authorization`
header to claw-interface. Cookie-authenticated BFF requests therefore
passed middleware while the backend still received no bearer token.

## Test plan

       expect(callArgs.headers['X-ECAP-Virtual-Key']).toBe('team-key-123')
     })
 
+    it('should forward zc_session cookie token as Authorization when original request has no Authorization', async () => {
+      process.env.CLAW_INTERFACE_URL = 'https://backend.test.com'
+      fetchSpy.mockResolvedValueOnce(new Response('{}', { status: 200 }))
+      const cookieToken = 'c'.repeat(40)
+
+      const originalRequest = new Request('http://localhost/api/test', {
+        headers: { cookie: `zc_session=${cookieToken}` },
+      })
+
+      const { proxyToBackend } = await import('@/lib/api/proxy')
+      await proxyToBackend('/test', undefined, { originalRequest })
+
+      const callArgs = fetchSpy.mock.calls[0][1]
+      expect(callArgs.headers['Authorization']).toBe(`Bearer ${cookieToken}`)
+    })
+
+    it('should prefer original Authorization over zc_session cookie token', async () => {
+      process.env.CLAW_INTERFACE_URL = 'https://backend.test.com'
+      fetchSpy.mockResolvedValueOnce(new Response('{}', { status: 200 }))
+
+      const originalRequest = new Request('http://localhost/api/test', {
+        headers: {
+          Authorization: 'Bearer explicit-token',
+          cookie: `zc_session=${'c'.repeat(40)}`,
+        },
+      })
+
+      const { proxyToBackend } = await import('@/lib/api/proxy')
+      await proxyToBackend('/test', undefined, { originalRequest })
+
+      const callArgs = fetchSpy.mock.calls[0][1]
+      expect(callArgs.headers['Authorization']).toBe('Bearer explicit-token')
+    })
+

commit.message:
fix(desktop): repair the pandaclaw desktop build chain (#2484)

## Problem

The **pandaclaw-desktop** packaging has been broken since the web app
moved from `web/` to `web/app` — the staging/server scripts still
referenced the old layout, so `pnpm build:mac` / `build:win` never
produced a working app (no `.web-stage` / `release` artifacts existed
anywhere).

Verified the full chain end-to-end after this fix: `DESKTOP_BUILD=1 next
build` → `tsup` → `stage-web` → `electron-builder --dir` emits
`PandaClaw.app` with the Next server correctly at
`Contents/Resources/web/app/server.js`.

## Fixes

| File | Change |
|---|---|
| `web/app/next.config.ts` | Set `outputFileTracingRoot` for the desktop
standalone build. Without it Next traces from the outermost lockfile
(`~/package-lock.json`) and emits
`standalone/source/zooclaw-desktop/.../server.js` — a deep, unlocatable
layout. With it: `standalone/app/server.js` + `standalone/node_modules`.
|
| `desktop/package.json` (`stage-web`) | Copy from `../web/app/.next`
(was `../web/.next`) into `.web-stage/app/` (was `.web-stage/web/`),
matching the standalone layout. |
| `desktop/scripts/flatten-pnpm.js` | Consolidate deps into
`.web-stage/app/node_modules` (was `web/`). |
| `desktop/main/next-server.ts` | Load the packaged server from
`resources/web/app/server.js` (was `resources/web/web/server.js`). |
| `desktop/scripts/prune-broken-symlinks.js` (new) | Strip the dangling
pnpm dev-dep symlinks (`shiki`/`typescript`) that Next's standalone
tracer leaves behind, so `rsync -L` no longer aborts staging with exit
23. Pure Node, so it also works on the Windows CI runner. |

## Why this PR

Prerequisite for adding desktop build CI in `zooclaw-desktop` (which
consumes this repo as a submodule) — that CI can only succeed once the
build chain itself works.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>

|---|---|

| `desktop/package.json` (`stage-web`) | Copy from `../web/app/.next`

@@ -6,7 +6,7 @@
   "scripts": {
     "dev": "tsx scripts/dev.ts",
     "dev:kill": "lsof -ti tcp:3000,tcp:3001,tcp:3002,tcp:3003 2>/dev/null | xargs kill -9 2>/dev/null; pkill -9 -f 'zooclaw-desktop/ecap-workspace'; true",
-    "stage-web": "rm -rf .web-stage && rsync -rL ../web/.next/standalone/ .web-stage/ && rsync -rL ../web/.next/static/ .web-stage/web/.next/static/ && rsync -rL ../web/public/ .web-stage/web/public/ && node scripts/flatten-pnpm.js",
+    "stage-web": "rm -rf .web-stage && node scripts/prune-broken-symlinks.js ../web/app/.next/standalone && rsync -rL ../web/app/.next/standalone/ .web-stage/ && rsync -rL ../web/app/.next/static/ .web-stage/app/.next/static/ && rsync -rL ../web/app/public/ .web-stage/app/public/ && node scripts/flatten-pnpm.js",
     "build": "rm -rf dist && tsup && pnpm stage-web && electron-builder",
     "build:mac": "rm -rf dist && tsup && pnpm stage-web && electron-builder --mac",
     "build:win": "rm -rf dist && tsup && pnpm stage-web && electron-builder --win",

@@ -1,3 +1,5 @@
+import path from 'node:path'
+
 import { withSentryConfig } from '@sentry/nextjs'
 import { config, populate } from 'dotenv'
 import type { NextConfig } from 'next'
@@ -92,6 +94,13 @@ const nextConfig: NextConfig = {
   ...(isDesktopBuild
     ? {
         output: 'standalone',
+        // Pin the trace root to the web workspace (this app lives at web/app and
+        // its deps hoist to web/node_modules). Without this Next walks up to the
+        // outermost lockfile (~/package-lock.json) and emits a deeply-nested
+        // standalone (standalone/source/zooclaw-desktop/.../server.js) that the
+        // desktop packager can't locate. With it, the layout is the predictable
+        // standalone/app/server.js + standalone/node_modules.
+        outputFileTracingRoot: path.join(process.cwd(), '..'),
       }
     : {}),

commit.message:
fix(claw-interface): use team billing credits (#2486)

## Summary

- Use the org billing team id when checking team workspace credits.
- Persist `avatar_url` on agent workspace install/update paths from pack
metadata.
- Return workspace `avatar_url` from `/openclaw/agents` and prefer it in
the sidebar when present.

## Tests

- `services/claw-interface/.venv/bin/python -m pytest
services/claw-interface/tests/unit/test_user_credits.py
services/claw-interface/tests/unit/test_agent_install_service.py
services/claw-interface/tests/unit/test_agent_multi_install_service.py
services/claw-interface/tests/unit/test_agent_update_service.py
services/claw-interface/tests/unit/test_agent_workspace_repo.py
services/claw-interface/tests/unit/test_agent_service.py
services/claw-interface/tests/unit/test_agent_response.py
services/claw-interface/tests/unit/test_openclaw_agents.py -q` (289
passed)
- `cd services/claw-interface && .venv/bin/ruff check .`
- `cd services/claw-interface && .venv/bin/ruff format --check .`
- `cd services/claw-interface && .venv/bin/pyright --pythonpath
.venv/bin/python app/ tests/`
- `cd services/claw-interface && .venv/bin/lint-imports`
- `bash scripts/verify-web.sh
web/app/src/components/sidenav/SideNavAgentList.tsx
web/app/src/hooks/useInstallingComputerAgents.ts
web/app/src/lib/api/openclaw-agents-v2-types.ts
web/app/src/lib/api/openclaw.ts
web/app/tests/unit/components/sidenav/SideNavAgentList.unit.spec.tsx`
could not complete locally because `web/node_modules` is missing (`tsc`,
`vitest`, and `eslint` commands not found).
- `bash scripts/verify-changed.sh` passed with skips only; it skipped
web due missing `web/node_modules` and skipped py because the script
could not find pyright/lint-imports on PATH, so backend checks were run
directly from the claw-interface venv above.

commit.message:
fix(web): skip web onboarding and add admin app link (#2487)

## Summary

- add a `Web App` entry to the enterprise-admin sidebar and mobile nav,
defaulting to `https://zooclaw.ai/new-chat`
- disable web app onboarding as a chat blocker for logged-in users
- update regression coverage for the admin app link and onboarding
resolver behavior

## Local Checks

- `bash scripts/verify-changed.sh`
- `pnpm --dir web/app exec vitest run
tests/unit/components/onboarding/resolveOnboardingStatus.unit.spec.ts`
- `pnpm --dir web/enterprise-admin exec vitest run
components/layout/__tests__/Sidebar.test.tsx`
- `pnpm --dir web/enterprise-admin exec tsc --noEmit`
- `pnpm --dir web/enterprise-admin exec eslint
components/layout/Sidebar.tsx components/layout/MobileNav.tsx
components/layout/__tests__/Sidebar.test.tsx`
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`

commit.message:
fix(web): add search & filters to agent pack and vertical plan management (#2457)

## What & Why

Improves management efficiency of the zooclaw admin console
(`dashboard-console`) by adding search/filtering and tidying column
semantics — across **both** catalogue surfaces: **Agent packs** and
**Vertical pack plans**.

## Agent packs

1. **Search box** — matches name, display id, version, category, short
bio, bio, status, and every skill / integration / automation tag,
case-insensitive.
2. **Market visibility filter** — `all` / `in market` / `hidden`.
3. **Default sort** — by `published_at` descending (newest first).
4. **Status filter** — defaults to `all` so a freshly created
draft/submitted pack is never hidden the moment its dialog closes;
`active` and the other statuses are available as filters.
5. **"Scope" column → short bio** — the list column now shows the pack's
short bio.
6. **"Hidden market" mark moved** — from the Scope column into the
**Status** column.

## Vertical pack plans

7. **Search box** — matches plan name, plan id, price, credits, and the
ids **and resolved display names** of included + add-on agent packs (so
searching the agent name shown in the table, e.g. "Agent Studio",
works), case-insensitive (reuses the parameterized `PackSearch`).
8. **Default sort** — by `updated_at` descending (most recently updated
first), so a just-edited plan surfaces at the top and matches the
table's "Updated" column. Applied at the view layer only; the canonical
`sortVerticalPackPlans` (name sort) used by the data layer and mutations
is untouched.

## Review follow-ups (codex)

- Reverted the agent-packs default from `active` → `all` (item 4): an
`active` default hid new draft/submitted packs after create, reading
like a failed action.
- Switched the plan sort from `created_at` → `updated_at` (item 8) so
recency tracks the last edit, consistent with the "Updated" column.

## Scope / safety

Presentation + **client-side** derivation only. No API, data-model, or
view-model behavior changes — `claw-api`, `packs`, `vertical-pack-plans`
lib, React Query wiring, and the live/seed fallback are untouched.
Filtering/sorting are pure module-level functions exercised directly by
unit tests.

## Verification

- `tsc -b` → 0 errors
- `eslint .` → 0 errors
- `vitest run` → 27 files / 200 tests pass, including view-model tests
for both surfaces (agent-packs: default-all newest-first, full-catalogue
sort, hidden/visible filter, multi-dimension search; plans: updated_at
sort proven by a created-earlier/edited-later case + multi-dimension
search).

---------

Co-authored-by: David Lu <davidlu@Daviddebijibendiannao.local>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(web): add search & filters to agent pack and vertical plan management (#2457)

## What & Why

Improves management efficiency of the zooclaw admin console
(`dashboard-console`) by adding search/filtering and tidying column
semantics — across **both** catalogue surfaces: **Agent packs** and
**Vertical pack plans**.

## Agent packs

1. **Search box** — matches name, display id, version, category, short
bio, bio, status, and every skill / integration / automation tag,
case-insensitive.
2. **Market visibility filter** — `all` / `in market` / `hidden`.
3. **Default sort** — by `published_at` descending (newest first).
4. **Status filter** — defaults to `all` so a freshly created
draft/submitted pack is never hidden the moment its dialog closes;
`active` and the other statuses are available as filters.
5. **"Scope" column → short bio** — the list column now shows the pack's
short bio.
6. **"Hidden market" mark moved** — from the Scope column into the
**Status** column.

## Vertical pack plans

7. **Search box** — matches plan name, plan id, price, credits, and the
ids **and resolved display names** of included + add-on agent packs (so
searching the agent name shown in the table, e.g. "Agent Studio",
works), case-insensitive (reuses the parameterized `PackSearch`).
8. **Default sort** — by `updated_at` descending (most recently updated
first), so a just-edited plan surfaces at the top and matches the
table's "Updated" column. Applied at the view layer only; the canonical
`sortVerticalPackPlans` (name sort) used by the data layer and mutations
is untouched.

## Review follow-ups (codex)

- Reverted the agent-packs default from `active` → `all` (item 4): an
`active` default hid new draft/submitted packs after create, reading
like a failed action.
- Switched the plan sort from `created_at` → `updated_at` (item 8) so
recency tracks the last edit, consistent with the "Updated" column.

## Scope / safety

Presentation + **client-side** derivation only. No API, data-model, or
view-model behavior changes — `claw-api`, `packs`, `vertical-pack-plans`
lib, React Query wiring, and the live/seed fallback are untouched.
Filtering/sorting are pure module-level functions exercised directly by
unit tests.

## Verification

- `tsc -b` → 0 errors
- `eslint .` → 0 errors
- `vitest run` → 27 files / 200 tests pass, including view-model tests
for both surfaces (agent-packs: default-all newest-first, full-catalogue
sort, hidden/visible filter, multi-dimension search; plans: updated_at
sort proven by a created-earlier/edited-later case + multi-dimension
search).

feat(enterprise-admin): split onboarding registration (#2485)

## Summary

- Add `registration_completed` to org responses and updates so
enterprise-admin can separate login from onboarding completion.
- Add `POST /account/team-org` for account registration plus team org
creation, with the new org starting incomplete.
- Update enterprise-admin signup to call `/account/team-org`; direct
signup continues to onboarding, while checkout-driven signup returns to
checkout and can purchase with an incomplete team org.

+@router.post(
+    "/team-org",
+    response_model=UserRegisterResponse,
+    status_code=status.HTTP_201_CREATED,
+)
+async def register_team_org_handler(
+    request: UserRegisterTeamOrgRequest,
+    identity: TokenIdentity = Depends(get_token_identity),
+) -> UserRegisterResponse:
+    """Register the caller and create/recover their team org."""
+    account, created, _ = await _register_account(identity=identity, metadata=request.metadata)
+
+    org = None
+    try:
+        org = await org_service.create_org(
+            name=request.name,
+            org_type="team",
+            created_by=account.uid,
+            default_computer_quota=request.default_computer_quota,
+            default_ephemeral_quota=request.default_ephemeral_quota,
+            warm_pool_size=request.warm_pool_size,
+            registration_completed=False,
+            logo_url=request.logo_url,
+        )
+    except DomainValidationError as exc:
+        if exc.code not in {"org.already_active_member", "org.already_a_member"}:
+            raise
+
+    membership = await membership_service.get_any_membership(account.uid)
+    if membership is None:
+        raise NotFoundError(
+            code="org.membership_not_found",
+            detail="Team org membership was not found after registration",
+            context={"uid": account.uid},
+        )
+    if org is None or org.org_id != membership.org_id:
+        org = await org_service.get_org(membership.org_id)
+    if org.org_type != "team":
+        raise DomainValidationError(
+            code="org.already_a_member",
+            detail="User already belongs to a non-team organization",
+            context={"existing_org_id": org.org_id, "org_type": org.org_type},
+        )
+    org_block = OrgBlock.from_org_and_membership(org, membership)
+    return UserRegisterResponse.from_account(account, created=created, org_block=org_block)
+
+

+from datetime import UTC, datetime
+from unittest.mock import AsyncMock
+
+import pytest
+
+from app.errors import DomainValidationError
+from app.routes import account as account_routes
+from app.schema.account import Account, AccountMetadata
+from app.schema.account_api import UserRegisterTeamOrgRequest
+from app.schema.account_org import AccountOrg
+from app.schema.auth import TokenIdentity
+from app.schema.org import Org
+
+
+@pytest.mark.asyncio
+async def test_register_team_org_creates_account_and_team_org(monkeypatch):
+    identity = TokenIdentity(uid="uid_1", email="admin@example.com", name="Admin")
+    metadata = AccountMetadata(created_from="enterprise_admin", platform="web")
+    account = Account(
+        uid=identity.uid,
+        email=identity.email,
+        name=identity.name,
+        billing_initialized=True,
+    )
+    now = datetime.now(UTC)
+    org = Org(
+        org_id="org_team_1",
+        name="Acme Inc",
+        org_type="team",
+        created_at=now,
+        created_by=identity.uid,
+        billing_team_id="tid_team_1",
+        default_computer_quota=7,
+        registration_completed=False,
+    )
+    membership = AccountOrg(
+        uid=identity.uid,
+        org_id=org.org_id,
+        role="admin",
+        joined_at=now,
+    )
+

+

commit.message:
docs(dashboard-console): fix dead account URL placeholder in .env.example (#2468)

Closes part of #2467 (the quick, low-risk piece).

## What

`web/dashboard-console/.env.example` shipped
`VITE_ACCOUNT_URL=https://account.ecap.yesy.live`, which is
**NXDOMAIN**. Anyone copying the example to `.env` and clicking
**Continue with Google** completes the Firebase popup and then fails the
token exchange with `ERR_NAME_NOT_RESOLVED`.

This PR:
1. Points `VITE_ACCOUNT_URL` at the staging account service
`https://account.favie.yesy.live` (same `*.yesy.live` family as the
existing `VITE_CLAW_INTERFACE_URL`; **verified by a successful local
login**), and notes prod is `https://account.gsmo.ai`.
2. Documents that `VITE_FIREBASE_*` are the `srp-ecap-staging` web
config values — the same ones `web/app` ships as
`NEXT_PUBLIC_FIREBASE_*`, just re-prefixed for Vite (only
`VITE_`-prefixed vars reach the browser). This rename was a non-obvious
gotcha when copying keys from web/app.

## Scope

Comment + placeholder change in a single `.env.example` template. No
code, no committed secrets (Firebase values stay blank in the template).

## Please confirm

`account.favie.yesy.live` was **inferred from the domain family +
DNS-verified + confirmed by a real login**, but the authoritative
staging hostname is owned by whoever runs the ecap deployment — please
sanity-check it. The broader doc/scripts work is tracked in #2467.

Co-authored-by: David Lu <davidlu@Daviddebijibendiannao.local>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -16,12 +16,19 @@
 # Requires CORS enabled on the backend for the dashboard origin.
 VITE_CLAW_INTERFACE_URL=https://claw-interface.ecap.yesy.live
 
-# Account-service base URL the BROWSER calls for email-OTP login + /user/me.
-# (Confirm against the ecap deployment; same service enterprise-app uses.)
-VITE_ACCOUNT_URL=https://account.ecap.yesy.live
+# Account-service base URL the BROWSER calls for email-OTP login + /user/me,
+# and the Firebase-token exchange (/auth/exchange) behind "Continue with Google".
+# Staging is on the *.yesy.live family (same as the claw-interface URL above);
+# production is https://account.gsmo.ai. Confirm the canonical host with the ecap
+# deployment owner before relying on it.
+VITE_ACCOUNT_URL=https://account.favie.yesy.live
 
 # Firebase web config for Google sign-in (exchanged for an account token via
 # auth-client's /auth/exchange). All VITE_-prefixed -> shipped in the client bundle.
+# These are the srp-ecap-staging web-app config values, also used by web/app — but
+# note web/app names them NEXT_PUBLIC_FIREBASE_* (Next.js); here they MUST be the
+# VITE_FIREBASE_* names below (Vite only exposes VITE_-prefixed vars). Same values,
+# different prefix. Leave blank to use email-OTP login only; fill in for Google.
 VITE_FIREBASE_API_KEY=
 VITE_FIREBASE_AUTH_DOMAIN=
 VITE_FIREBASE_PROJECT_ID=

commit.message:
feat(openclaw): add computer lifecycle backend (#2471)

## Summary
- add backend computer lifecycle support for create/list/start/redeploy
flows
- move OpenClaw computer creation into a dedicated service used by
account registration and explicit computer creation
- add unit coverage for account initialization, computer routes, and
computer services

## Local Checks
- `git diff --check -- services/claw-interface` passed
- `services/claw-interface/.venv/bin/python -m py_compile ...` passed
for changed backend files
- `bash scripts/verify-py.sh` passes `ruff check` and `ruff format
--check`; local run cannot complete because `pyright` and `lint-imports`
are not installed in this environment
- targeted `services/claw-interface/.venv/bin/python -m pytest ...`
cannot run because `.venv` has no `pytest`
- targeted `uv run pytest ...` is blocked by the existing editable build
parsing issue for
`git+https://github.com/SerendipityOneInc/favie-common.git@v0.3.66`

## Notes
- Split from #2470 so backend lifecycle changes can review independently
from the frontend init-route removal.

+"""FastClaw bot creation helpers for the normalized computer create flow."""
+
+from __future__ import annotations
+
+import logging
+
+from app.schema.account import Account
+from app.schema.computer import (
+    CreatedFastClawBot,
+    CreateFastClawBotRequest,
+    PreparedFastClawBotCreate,
+)
+from app.services import mattermost_provisioner
+from app.services.agent_identity import create_agent_email_with_retry
+from app.services.computer import bot_state_service
+from app.services.openclaw import bot_lifecycle
+from app.services.openclaw_client import get_openclaw_client
+from app.settings import PLAN_RESOURCES
+
+logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
+
+
+class MissingFastClawBillingKeyError(RuntimeError):
+    """Raised when a bot create path does not have an account-bound billing key."""
+
+
+async def prepare_account_agent_email(account: Account, user_categories: list[str] | None) -> dict | None:
+    """Return an existing or newly-created account agent email without persisting it."""
+    agent_email_info = account.agent_email
+    if agent_email_info and agent_email_info.get("email_address"):
+        return agent_email_info
+    try:
+        return await create_agent_email_with_retry(user_categories or [], account.uid)
+    except Exception as exc:
+        logger.warning("[OPENCLAW] Agent email creation failed (non-blocking) - uid=%s, error=%s", account.uid[:8], exc)
+        return None
+
+
+async def prepare_account_fastclaw_bot_create(
+    *,
+    account: Account,
+    app_token: str,
+    bot_name: str,
+    user_token: str,
+    user_categories: list[str] | None,
+) -> PreparedFastClawBotCreate:
+    """Prepare account-backed FastClaw bot creation dependencies."""
+    litellm_api_key = account.billing_key or ""
+    if not litellm_api_key:
+        raise MissingFastClawBillingKeyError("billing_key missing before bot creation")
+
+    agent_email_info = await prepare_account_agent_email(account, user_categories)
+    account_plan = account.plan or "free"
+    bot_resources = PLAN_RESOURCES.get(account_plan, PLAN_RESOURCES["free"])
+
+    mm_channel_config: dict | None = None
+    mm_early_result = None
+    try:
+        mm_early_result = await mattermost_provisioner.provision_mattermost_early(account.uid, bot_name)
+        mm_channel_config = mattermost_provisioner.build_mm_channel_config("default", mm_early_result.bot_token)
+        logger.info("[OPENCLAW] MM early provision done - uid=%s", account.uid)
+    except Exception as exc:
+        logger.warning("[OPENCLAW] MM early provision failed, will retry later - uid=%s, error=%s", account.uid, exc)
+
+    return PreparedFastClawBotCreate(
+        request=CreateFastClawBotRequest(
+            uid=account.uid,
+            bot_name=bot_name,
+            app_token=app_token,
+            team_key=litellm_api_key,
+            user_token=user_token,
+            agent_email_info=agent_email_info,
+            resources=bot_resources,
+            channels=mm_channel_config,
+        ),
+        litellm_api_key=litellm_api_key,
+        agent_email_info=agent_email_info,
+        mattermost_result=mm_early_result,
+    )
+
+
+async def create_fastclaw_bot(
+    request: CreateFastClawBotRequest,
+    *,
+    allow_app_reset: bool = True,
+) -> CreatedFastClawBot:
+    """Create a FastClaw bot and normalize the response for callers."""
+    client = get_openclaw_client()
+    app_token = request.app_token
+    try:
+        result = await _create_fastclaw_bot(client, request, app_token=app_token)
+    except Exception as create_err:
+        if "401" not in str(create_err):
+            raise
+        if not allow_app_reset:
+            raise
+        logger.warning(
+            "[OPENCLAW] 401 on normalized create_bot, resetting app token and retrying - uid=%s", request.uid
+        )
+        app_token = await _refresh_app_for_create_retry(request.uid)
+        result = await _create_fastclaw_bot(client, request, app_token=app_token)
+
+    bot_id = result.get("id", "")
+    if not bot_id:
+        raise RuntimeError("FastClaw create_bot returned an empty bot id")
+
+    return CreatedFastClawBot(
+        bot_id=bot_id,
+        access_token=result.get("access_token") or "",
+        app_token=app_token,
+        raw=result,
+    )
+
+

+async def cleanup_mattermost_provision_result(
+    uid: str,
+    result: MattermostProvisionResult | None,
+    *,
+    reason: str,
+) -> None:
+    """Best-effort cleanup for Mattermost resources created before bot persistence."""
+    if result is None:
+        return
+
+    try:
+        mm_client = get_mattermost_client()
+    except Exception as exc:
+        logger.warning(
+            "[MATTERMOST] Early provision cleanup failed - uid=%s reason=%s action=get_client error=%s",
+            uid,
+            reason,
+            exc,
+        )
+        return
+
+    async def run(label: str, action) -> None:
+        try:
+            await action
+        except Exception as exc:
+            logger.warning(
+                "[MATTERMOST] Early provision cleanup failed - uid=%s reason=%s action=%s error=%s",
+                uid,
+                reason,
+                label,
+                exc,
+            )
+
+    bot_token_id = getattr(result, "bot_token_id", "")
+    if bot_token_id:
+        await run("revoke_bot_token", mm_client.revoke_user_token(bot_token_id))
+
+    mm_user_token_id = getattr(result, "mm_user_token_id", "")
+    if mm_user_token_id:
+        await run("revoke_user_token", mm_client.revoke_user_token(mm_user_token_id))
+
+    if result.dm_channel_id:
+        await run("delete_dm_channel", mm_client.delete_channel(result.dm_channel_id))
+
+    if result.bot_user_id:
+        await run("deactivate_bot_user", mm_client.update_user_active(result.bot_user_id, False))
+
+    if getattr(result, "mm_user_newly_created", False) and result.mm_user_id:
+        await run("deactivate_mm_user", mm_client.update_user_active(result.mm_user_id, False))
+

+from types import SimpleNamespace
+from unittest.mock import AsyncMock, patch
+
+import pytest
+
+from app.schema.account import Account
+from app.schema.computer import CreateFastClawBotRequest
+from app.services.computer.computer_create_service import (
+    MissingFastClawBillingKeyError,
+    create_fastclaw_bot,
+    prepare_account_fastclaw_bot_create,
+)
+
+
+@pytest.mark.asyncio
+async def test_create_fastclaw_bot_returns_created_bot_and_passes_optional_payload():
+    mock_client = AsyncMock()
+    mock_client.create_bot = AsyncMock(return_value={"id": "bot-1", "access_token": "access-token"})
+
+    with patch("app.services.computer.computer_create_service.get_openclaw_client", return_value=mock_client):
+        result = await create_fastclaw_bot(
+            CreateFastClawBotRequest(
+                uid="uid-1",
+                bot_name="Main",
+                app_token="app-token",
+                team_key="team-key",
+                user_token="user-token",
+                agent_email_info={"email_address": "agent@example.com"},
+                resources={"cpu_request": "100m"},
+                channels={"default": {"enabled": True}},
+            )
+        )
+
+    assert result.bot_id == "bot-1"
+    assert result.access_token == "access-token"
+    assert result.raw == {"id": "bot-1", "access_token": "access-token"}
+    mock_client.create_bot.assert_awaited_once_with(
+        "uid-1",
+        "Main",
+        app_token="app-token",
+        team_key="team-key",
+        user_token="user-token",
+        agent_email_info={"email_address": "agent@example.com"},
+        resources={"cpu_request": "100m"},
+        channels={"default": {"enabled": True}},
+    )
+
+
+@pytest.mark.asyncio
+async def test_create_fastclaw_bot_retries_with_fresh_app_on_401_when_reset_allowed():
+    mock_client = AsyncMock()
+    mock_client.create_bot = AsyncMock(
+        side_effect=[
+            RuntimeError("401 Unauthorized"),
+            {"id": "bot-2", "access_token": "fresh-access-token"},
+        ]
+    )
+    mock_client.create_app = AsyncMock(return_value={"id": "app-2", "api_token": "fresh-app-token"})
+
+    with (
+        patch("app.services.computer.computer_create_service.get_openclaw_client", return_value=mock_client),
+        patch(
+            "app.services.computer.bot_state_service.clear_bot_state",
+            new=AsyncMock(),
+        ) as clear_bot_state,
+        patch(
+            "app.services.openclaw.bot_lifecycle.ensure_app",
+            new=AsyncMock(return_value={"uid": "uid-1", "openclaw_app": {"api_token": "fresh-app-token"}}),
+        ) as ensure_app,
+        patch(
+            "app.database.openclaw_repo.clear_openclaw_state",
+            new=AsyncMock(),
+        ) as clear_openclaw_state,

+async def test_create_computer_persists_mattermost_state_returned_by_prepare():

+    assert mirror_args.args[0] == "uid_1"
+    assert mirror_args.args[1] == "main"

+    assert mm_bot["agent_id"] == "main"
+    assert mm_bot["account_id"] == "default"
+    assert mm_bot["bot_user_id"] == "mm-bot-user"
+    assert mm_bot["bot_username"] == "mm-bot-name"
+    assert mm_bot["bot_token"] == "mm-bot-token"
+    assert mm_bot["dm_channel_id"] == "dm-channel"
+    assert mm_bot["display_name"] == "Main"
+    assert mm_bot["enabled"] is True
+    assert mm_bot["channel_injected"] is False
+    assert mm_bot["created_at"]
+    assert mm_bot["updated_at"]
+    assert mirror_args.kwargs["computer_id"] == "bot_created"
+

+    get_computer = AsyncMock(return_value=_computer("bot_1", status="ready"))
+

commit.message:
feat(landing-context): deliver PPT Master Remix cover image as a chat attachment (#2466)

## What

Completes the **PPT Master "Remix"** hand-off on the chat side: a picked
template's **cover screenshot** is now delivered to the agent as a real
Mattermost **attachment** (not just a URL in the prompt text), sent
imperceptibly through the existing landing auto-send flow.

Producer side ships in `zooclaw-tips`
(SerendipityOneInc/zooclaw-tips#54): the gallery writes
`initial_query.images = [coverUrl]` + `auto_send` into
`ecap:landingContext`. This PR makes the consumer honor both.

Two commits:

1. **Carry the contract** — `landing-context.ts` previously rebuilt the
stored context as `initial_query: { text }` only, silently dropping any
producer-supplied `images` / `auto_send`. Now both are preserved
(sanitized: string-only image entries, capped at 8; `auto_send` accepts
only literal `true`), attached only when present so text-only hand-offs
keep their minimal shape.
2. **Deliver the images** — wire `initial_query.images` through the
auto-send path so they upload + attach to the message.

## How

- `blob.ts` — `fetchExternalBlob()` pulls a third-party image
**without** bearer auth (never leak the MM token to a foreign origin;
subject to the remote's CORS).
- `useMmAttachments` — `uploadExternalImageUrls()` fetches each URL,
uploads to the channel, returns file ids; isolated from the composer's
attachment UI state (the message is programmatic, no preview chips).
- `useChatMessaging` — `handleSendMessage()` accepts explicit `fileIds`
(bypasses the render-lagged attachment-state consumption that would drop
a same-tick upload); `handleSendMessageWithImages()` uploads then sends
text + ids.
- `useLandingContextFlow` / `GenClawClient` — thread images through
`onAutoSendInitialQuery`, passing them only when present.

## Notes

- **Best-effort images**: if every upload fails (CORS, network), it
falls back to sending text alone — the cover URL is already embedded in
the prompt text, so the agent isn't left empty-handed.
- **CORS caveat**: browser must fetch the image bytes to re-upload, so
delivery depends on the image origin's CORS. `raw.githubusercontent.com`
returns `ACAO:*` (works); other origins without CORS headers degrade to
text-only. A server-side proxy would remove this dependency (out of
scope here).

## Test

- `tsc --noEmit` clean.
- 132 unit tests pass across `landing-context`, `useMmAttachments`,
`useChatMessaging`, `useLandingContextFlow` (incl. new images-carry +
images-forwarding tests).

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

+// Pass images only when present so text-only hand-offs keep the single-arg
+// call shape (and the existing call contract). Kept module-level so the poll
+// callback stays under its complexity budget.
+function invokeAutoSend(
+  send: (text: string, images?: string[]) => Promise<boolean>,
+  text: string,
+  images: string[] | undefined,
+): Promise<boolean> {
+  return images && images.length > 0 ? send(text, images) : send(text)
+}

@@ -28,3 +28,16 @@ export async function fetchMmBlob(url: string, token: string, signal?: AbortSign
   if (!res.ok) throw new Error(`HTTP ${res.status}`)
   return res.blob()
 }
+
+/**
+ * Fetch a third-party (non-MM) URL as a Blob WITHOUT bearer auth — used to pull
+ * a producer-supplied remote image (e.g. a landing hand-off cover screenshot)
+ * into the browser so it can be re-uploaded to Mattermost. Never send the MM
+ * token to a foreign origin; subject to the remote's CORS policy, so callers
+ * must treat a throw as "skip this image" and fall back to text-only.
+ */
+export async function fetchExternalBlob(url: string, signal?: AbortSignal): Promise<Blob> {
+  const res = await fetch(url, { signal })
+  if (!res.ok) throw new Error(`HTTP ${res.status}`)
+  return res.blob()
+}

commit.message:
fix(billing): reduce expected invoice and user 404 noise (#2297)

## Summary
- Add backend `invoice_available` to order responses so invoice download
visibility follows provider invoice availability.
- Hide invoice Download for paid Stripe rows without a hosted invoice
id.
- Retry transient `/users/get` 404s during login bootstrap from the
current React Query user-business-data path.
- Mark only intermediate `/api/users/get` retry attempts for Sentry
filtering; an exhausted final 404 remains visible.
- Drop handled invoice-download 404 noise from Sentry for the precise
invoice endpoint pattern.

## Root cause
Paid Stripe order rows were rendered as downloadable based only on
`payment_channel === "stripe"`, but some renewal/payment rows do not yet
carry `provider_invoice_id`, so clicking Download correctly fell through
to `/orders/{orderId}/invoice` 404.

Separately, `/api/users/get` can briefly return 404 during account
bootstrap. The UI should retry those transient responses, but persistent
404s still need to reach Sentry because they may indicate a real missing
business-user record.

## Review follow-up
- Rebased the PR onto the latest `main` and removed the stale conflict
with the retired `user-business-data-cache.ts` module.
- Migrated the bootstrap 404 retry behavior into
`web/app/src/lib/api/user-business-data.ts`, the current shared React
Query query function.
- Kept the Codex review boundary: only explicitly marked intermediate
retry attempts use `ecap_expected_404=user_bootstrap_retry`;
unmarked/final `/api/users/get` 404s are still reported.
- Made the retry backoff abort-aware so `cancelQueries` does not wait
for the 300/800/1500ms delay before observing cancellation.
- Disabled the outer React Query retry for the user-business-data hook
and imperative `fetchQuery` paths, so the internal 4-attempt bootstrap
window does not run twice under the global `retry: 1` default.
- Kept `invoice_available` as the backend-owned invariant. Frontend uses
strict `=== true`, so old/undefined responses degrade to hiding a broken
Download button instead of showing one that 404s.

## Test plan
- [x] `bash scripts/verify-web.sh web/app/src/lib/api/user.ts
web/app/src/lib/api/user-business-data.ts
web/app/src/contexts/UserBusinessDataContext.tsx
web/app/src/components/billing/InvoiceHistory.tsx
web/app/sentry.client.config.ts
web/app/tests/unit/lib/api/user.unit.spec.ts
web/app/tests/unit/lib/api/user-business-data.unit.spec.ts
web/app/tests/unit/contexts/UserBusinessDataContext.unit.spec.tsx
web/app/tests/unit/components/billing/InvoiceHistory.unit.spec.tsx
web/app/tests/unit/config/sentry-client-config.unit.spec.ts`
- [x] `pnpm --filter @zooclaw/web-app exec vitest run --config
./vitest.config.mts tests/unit/lib/api/user-business-data.unit.spec.ts
tests/unit/contexts/UserBusinessDataContext.unit.spec.tsx`
- [x]
`/Users/xuwenhao/Codebase/srpone/zooclaw/ecap-workspace/services/claw-interface/.venv/bin/python
-m ruff check app/schema/order.py
app/services/billing_v2/order_requests.py
tests/unit/test_billing_v2_order_requests.py`
- [x]
`/Users/xuwenhao/Codebase/srpone/zooclaw/ecap-workspace/services/claw-interface/.venv/bin/python
-m ruff format --check app/schema/order.py
app/services/billing_v2/order_requests.py
tests/unit/test_billing_v2_order_requests.py`
- [x]
`/Users/xuwenhao/Codebase/srpone/zooclaw/ecap-workspace/services/claw-interface/.venv/bin/python

app/services/billing_v2/order_requests.py
tests/unit/test_billing_v2_order_requests.py --pythonpath
/Users/xuwenhao/Codebase/srpone/zooclaw/ecap-workspace/services/claw-interface/.venv/bin/python`
